rulings.Case Title: LEIASA BECKHAM VS. KASLOFSKY & ASSOCIATES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT 1850 BRYANT LAND LLC, CHRISTOPHER FOLEY, DOUGLAS ROSS DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead the causes of action subject to this demurrer with requisite particularity (facts, not conclusions) and to allege facts in support of the allegation of fiduciary duty. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Amended Motion And Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery From Defendants Lem Holdings, Llc, Ciaran Harty, And Patrick Harty And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF TAL TAMIR, CELINE TAMIR Notice Of Amended Motion And Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery From Defendants Lem Holdings, Llc, Ciaran Harty, And Patrick Harty And For Monetary Sanctions IS GRANTED AS SET FORTH BELOW. (1) DEVELOPER DEFENDANTS' BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS 1 -46, THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 1 - 10 AND FORM INTERROGATORIES TO LEM NOS. 301.1 - 301.7, 304.1 - 304.3, 311.1 -311.4, 314.1 - 314.7, 321.1 - 321.13, 322.1 - 322.4 AND 325.1 - 325.4 , AND FORM INTERROGATORIES TO PATRICK/CIARAN NOS 303.1, 303.6 - 303.7, 304.1 -304.3, 311.1 - 311.4, 312.1, 314.1 - 314.7, 321.1 - 321.13 322.1 - 322.4, AND 325.1 - 325.4 ARE OVERRULED. (2) BROKER DEFENDANTS WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS CODE COMPLIANT VERIFIED RESPONSES TO EACH OF THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. TO THE EXTENT THAT BROKER DEFENDANTS SEEKS TO WITHOLD ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ON THE BASIS OF A CLAIM OF A/C PRIVILEGE IT WILL IDENTIFY THE WITHELD DOCUMENTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALLOW PLAINTIFFS TO CHALLENCE THE A/C CLAIM. TO THE EXTENT THAT BROKER DEFENDANT RESPONSE INCLUDE DOCUMENTS PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED IT NEED NOT REPRODUCE THE DOCUMENT, BUT IS REQUIRED TO IDENTIFY IT WITH SPECIFICTY IN RESPONSE TO THE APPROPRIATE REQUEST. (3) BROKER DEFENDANTS WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FRIN TGE DATE IF THIS ORDER CODE COMPLIANT VERIFIED RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 1 - 10. TO THE EXTENT THAT BROKER DEFENDANTS ASSERT THAT A FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSE INVOLVES THE DISCLOSURE OF A/C INFORMATION, IT WILL MAKE SUCH OBJECTION WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIIFICITY TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM. (4) LEM WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER CODE COMPLIANT VERIFIED RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS 301.1, 303.1 - 303.7, 304.1 - 304.3, 311.1 - 311.4, 312, 314.1 - 314.7, 321.1 - 314.7, 321.1 - 321.13, 322.1 - 322.4, 325.1 - 325.4 (5) PATRICK/CIARAN WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER CODE COMPLIANT VERIFIED RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS. 303.1, 303.6 - 303.7, 304.1 - 304.3, 311.1 - 311.4, 312.1, 314.1 - 314.7, 321.1 - 321.13, 322.1 - 322.4 AND 325.1 - 325.4 (6) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY AGAINST ALL DEVELOPER DEFENDANTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500.00 WHICH WILL BE DUE AND PAYABLE TO PLAINTIFFS AND THEIR ATTORNEYS WITHIN TEN (10) FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Amended Motion And Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery From Defendants Apr Real Estate Services, Inc., Compass California Ii, Inc., And Paula Y. Rose And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2022 line 2. PLAINTIFF TAL TAMIR, CELINE TAMIR Notice Of Amended Motion And Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery From Defendants Apr Real Estate Services, Inc., Compass California Ii, Inc., And Paula Y. Rose And For Monetary Sanctions IS GRANTED AS SET FORTH BELOW. (1) BROKER DEFENDANTS BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS 1 -36, THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 1 - 8 AND FORM INTERROGATORIES TO APR/COMPASS NOS.3.1, 3.7, 4.1 -4.2, 12.1 - 12.7, 13.1 - 13.2, 14.1 - 14.2. 50.1 - 50.6. ARE OVERRULED. (2) BROKER DEFENDANTS BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS 1 -36, THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 1 - 8 AND FORM INTERROGATORIES TO APR/COMPASS NOS.3.1, 3.7, 4.1 -4.2, 12.1 - 12.7, 13.1 - 13.2, 14.1 - 14.2. 50.1 - 50.6. ARE OVERRULED. (3) BROKER DEFENDANTS WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS CODE COMPLIANT VERIFIED RESPONSES TO EACH OF THE REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. TO THE EXTENT THAT BROKER DEFENDANTS SEEKS TO WITHOLD ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS ON THE BASIS OF A CLAIM OF A/C PRIVILEGE IT WILL IDENTIFY THE WITHELD DOCUMENTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALL PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENCE THE A/C CLAIM. TO THE EXTENT THAT BROKER DEFENDANT RESPONSE INCLUDE DOCUMENTS PREVIOUSLY PRODUCE IT NEED NOT REPRODUCE THE DOCUMENT, BUT IS REQUIRED TO IDENTIFY IT WITH SPECIFICTY IN RESPONSE TO THE APPROPRIATE REQUEST. (4) BROKER DEFENDANTS WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FRIN TGE DATE IF THIS ORDER CODE COMPLIANT VERIFIED RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 1 - 8. TO THE EXTENT THAT BROKER DEFENDANTS ASSERT THAT A FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSE INVOLVES THE DISCLOSURE OF A/C INFORMATION, IT WILL MAKE SUCH OBJECTION WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIIFICITY TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM. (5) APR/COMPASS WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER CODE COMPLIANT VERIFIED RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO FORM INTERROGATORIES NOS 3.1 - 3.7, 4.1 - 4.2, 12.1 - 12.7, 13.1 - 13.2, 14.1 - 14.2, 50.1 - 50.6. (6) ROSE WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS WITHIN TWENTY (20) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER CODE COMPLIANT VERIFIED RESPONSE, WITHOUT OBJECTION, TO FORM INTRROATORIES NOS. 2.1 - 2.3, 2.5 - 2.7, 2.9 - 2.13, 3.6 - 3.7, 4.1 - 4.2, 12.1 = 12./, 13.1 -13.2, 14.1 = 14.2, 50.1 - 50.6. (7) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS, JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY AGAINST ALL BROKER DEFENDANTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,500.00. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
